title: Atlas Architecture Memory
intro: A live-readable map of Atlas's own code, infrastructure, memory layers, roadmap, and known gaps.

status.label: Runtime
status.detail: VPS runtime handles Farcaster webhooks, Atlas brain prompts, direct replies, and brain API actions.

status.label: Campaign lifecycle
status.detail: Cloudflare Workflow advances durable campaign stages; runtime handles synthesis and evaluation work.

status.label: Architecture memory
status.detail: Backfilled as markdown and audit rows; loaded into Atlas self-context as compact current architecture.

status.label: Code diff awareness
status.detail: Atlas does not inspect GitHub, PRs, local git history, or raw code diffs unless explicit artifacts are supplied.

memoryLayers.name: Durable world memory
memoryLayers.purpose: Beliefs about the world changed only by reviewed Looti-ranked campaign evidence.

memoryLayers.name: Contributor memory
memoryLayers.purpose: Who contributed, what they answered, where they ranked, and whether prior contributions held up.

memoryLayers.name: Active campaign memory
memoryLayers.purpose: Live contributor snapshots and selected follow-up conversation state during collection.

memoryLayers.name: KG profile memory
memoryLayers.purpose: Generated profiles for the operator and top campaign contributors.

memoryLayers.name: Architecture memory
memoryLayers.purpose: Atlas's own code, infra, capabilities, deploys, failure modes, and known gaps.

items: Keep architecture memory in world/architecture and load the compact current snapshot into Atlas self-context.
Use active campaign memory so Atlas can see live contributor answers before campaign resolution.
Use pre-publish leases to reduce duplicate Atlas posts and duplicate contributor replies.
Create code_change_artifact records for meaningful deploys, commits, and PR merges.
Generate architecture-memory updates automatically on deploy and PR merge.
Expose the latest architecture events on this page from structured data instead of hand-written JSON.
Link code contributors to Farcaster identities.
Use campaign track record and linked identity as a pre-qualification layer for PR rights.
Let Atlas weigh proposed memory/code changes against contributor history.

incidents.name: Campaign launch order
incidents.summary: Atlas created a Looti campaign before casting the public question. Launch now casts first and uses the real Farcaster cast hash.

incidents.name: Budget fallback
incidents.summary: One path used a tiny 5 ATL fallback. Live campaign paths now use configured campaign budget defaults.

incidents.name: Contributor memory missing
incidents.summary: Ranked answers were stored without contributor/reputation upserts. Top ranked winners now upsert contributor memory and reputation.

incidents.name: Live campaign awareness missing
incidents.summary: Atlas did not know @ellis had already answered in another thread. Active campaign awareness is now loaded into relevant normal replies.

incidents.name: Duplicate public posts
incidents.summary: Some public side effects were recorded only after publishing. Atlas now claims action leases before high-risk publishing paths.

incidents.name: Code diff awareness
incidents.summary: Atlas spoke too loosely about reading diffs. Architecture memory now states that code/PR awareness requires explicit artifacts.

knownGaps: Architecture updates are not yet generated automatically from PRs or deploys.
Raw Farcaster payloads are not archived for every conversation.
Contributor conversation memory is selective and not every social chat becomes durable memory.
Code-change authorship is not yet linked to Farcaster identity or contributor reputation.
PR rights and code-review weighting are not implemented.
